# ☾☽ MOONSHINE

## Getting Started

- **What:** a Rust crate for Moonshine/Gnosis mesh and runtime experiments.
- **Why:** it keeps this experimental runtime lane separate from the more general Gnosis runtime and polyglot surfaces.
- **How:** use the crate commands in this README and treat the surface as experimental unless a section marks it production-ready.
- **Next:** read the scope, commands, and tests before connecting it to mesh workflows.

### Monster On Shell In Engine

```text
▗▖  ▗▖ ▗▄▖  ▗▄▖ ▗▖  ▗▖ ▗▄▄▖▗▖ ▗▖▗▄▄▄▖▗▖  ▗▖▗▄▄▄▖
▐▛▚▞▜▌▐▌ ▐▌▐▌ ▐▌▐▛▚▖▐▌▐▌   ▐▌ ▐▌  █  ▐▛▚▖▐▌▐▌   
▐▌  ▐▌▐▌ ▐▌▐▌ ▐▌▐▌ ▝▜▌ ▝▀▚▖▐▛▀▜▌  █  ▐▌ ▝▜▌▐▛▀▀▘
▐▌  ▐▌▝▚▄▞▘▝▚▄▞▘▐▌  ▐▌▗▄▄▞▘▐▌ ▐▌▗▄█▄▖▐▌  ▐▌▐▙▄▄▖
```

Moonshine is an autonomic, high-fidelity shell replacement designed for the Gnosis monorepo. It replaces linear script execution with a **topological engine** based on the fork/race/fold (FRF) paradigm, optimized for sub-millisecond orchestration latency.

---

## Quick Start: Gnosis Installer

Moonshine is optional in the user-facing Gnosis installer:

```bash
cd open-source/gnosis
./install.sh --with-moonshine
```

```powershell
cd open-source\gnosis
.\install.ps1 -WithMoonshine
```

Use this path when installing `moonshine` together with `gg`, `gnode`,
`monster`, and `gnexec`.

## Legacy Unix Installer

The easiest way to install Moonshine on any Unix-like machine (macOS/Linux) is using the magic installer. This script builds the optimized binary, initializes your autonomic identity, and sets up your shell aliases in one step.

```bash
# Clone the repository and run the installer
cd open-source/gnosis/moonshine
./moonshine.sh
```

**What the installer does:**
1.  **Builds** the optimized Rust binary (`cargo build --release`).
2.  **Initializes** your local DID (Decentralized Identifier) and UCAN foundation.
3.  **Injects** the `moonshine` alias into your `.zshrc`, `.bashrc`, or `.profile`.
4.  **Verifies** your environment with a diagnostic `moonshine doctor` run.

---

## 🛠 Manual Installation

If you prefer to install manually or are integrating Moonshine into a custom CI/CD pipeline:

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Rust Toolchain**: `curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh`

### 2. Build & Setup
```bash
cargo build --release
cp ./target/release/moonshine /usr/local/bin/ # Optional: Install to PATH
```

### 3. Initialize Identity
Moonshine requires a local identity to sign orchestration knots.
```bash
moonshine init
```

---

## ☾☽ Core Concepts

### 1. Topological Execution
Moonshine doesn't just run lines of code; it walks a **Control Flow Graph (CFG)**.
- `cmd1; cmd2` → **Sequential**: Stage 2 waits for Stage 1.
- `cmd1 & cmd2` → **Fork**: Both run in parallel.
- `{ race; cmd1; cmd2; }` → **Race**: First to finish wins; the loser is cancelled.
- `cmd1 | cmd2` → **Pipe**: Standard Unix piping via high-performance Rust channels.

### 2. Identity Encryption (Shadowing)
Moonshine implements **Prismatic Refraction**. Every process name and argument is XOR-obfuscated in diagnostic logs using your DID. Your system logs will show the "shadow" of the process, while the topological engine maintains the "truth."

### 3. Shadow Mode
Run sensitive commands in **Shadow Mode** to cast a thermodynamic trace while keeping the execution pristine.
```bash
moonshine -c "shadow echo 'Secret Command'"
```
**Output:**
```text
▞▞▞ [SHADOW] z010a0c554b290c420f44344b3e3f...
Secret Command
✦ 0.000214 μJ thermodynamic shadow cast
```

### 4. Facets
Facets are Moonshine's browser/Vim-fluent shell contexts. A facet is a fast
forkable execution cursor with its own label, last command, children, and working
directory snapshot. Forking creates two children and moves you into the left
child; switching is an open-ring pointer move, not a scan over history.

```bash
moonshine -c "!echo run this in a fresh facet"
moonshine -c "facet list"
```

Interactive controls:

- `!cmd` forks a fresh facet and runs `cmd` there.
- `facet fork`, `facet next`, `facet prev`, `facet close`, `facet close others`, `facet close children`, `facet list`
- Browser-style readline bindings: `Ctrl-T` forks, `Ctrl-W` closes, `Ctrl-Tab` moves next, `Ctrl-Shift-Tab` moves previous when the terminal passes those chords through.

---

## 🖥 Virtual Machines (`vm`)

Moonshine is a first-class front door to **sovereign microVMs**. The same runner
that regenerates admitted FOIL topologies on the vCPU also runs *opaque* payloads
faithfully — a native binary, an OCI image, or a **bootable `.iso`** — on a real
macOS Virtualization.framework microVM (Apple Silicon, arm64), every run recorded
by a braid certificate. See `man vm`.

```sh
vm boot  <target>                 # boot a VM to its live console (raw-mode TUIs work)
vm serve <target> [port] [--lan]  # boot + serve the console on the web (xterm.js)
vm run   <target> -- <command>    # run a braid-CERTIFIED command inside the guest
vm ls                             # list running VMs (PID + image)
vm kill  <pid|all>                # stop a VM
```

A **target** is one of three things — that's the whole UX:

- a **local path** — `vm boot ~/Downloads/ubuntu-24.04-live-server-arm64.iso`
- an **`http(s)://` URL** — `vm boot https://edge.forkjoin.ai/.../guest.iso`
  (downloaded once to a cache, then booted like a local file)
- an **`aeon://iso/<id>`** sovereign address — resolves to its R2/CDN object by id

### VMs live on the web

`vm serve` boots a VM and serves its console as a **WebSocket + an xterm.js page on
one port**:

```sh
vm serve ~/Downloads/ubuntu-24.04-live-server-arm64.iso 8723
# → open http://127.0.0.1:8723/ in any browser: a live terminal into the VM
vm serve aeon://iso/ubuntu-server 8723 --lan
# → --lan binds 0.0.0.0 so a phone / another device on your network can connect
```

This is the duplex transport that lets a Mac-hosted VM be driven from any
browser, phone, or app surface (aeon-shell / aeon-forge) — a WebSocket carries
the guest console out and keystrokes back, with a 256 KB scrollback replayed to
each new client so it sees the boot, not just what streams after it connects.

### Run a certified job inside an image

```sh
vm run ~/Downloads/alpine-virt-aarch64.iso -- 'cat /etc/os-release | head -1; apk --version'
# [cert 01e831…] REAL VZ boot; OpenRC up; localhost login: root
# --- guest output ---
# NAME="Alpine Linux"
# apk-tools 3.0.6-r0, compiled for aarch64.
```

The shims **`boot-iso`**, **`serve-iso`** (on `$PATH`) are the same thing one word
shorter. In **Moonshine.app**, the same targets boot as persistent **VM tabs**
(each its own microVM, kept running in the background), and `.iso` / `aeon://iso/…`
open as VMs via the OS deep-link handler.

> Booting is macOS + Apple Silicon, arm64 images (an x86_64 image gets a clear
> error; a desktop/GUI image won't render on a serial console — use a *server*
> image). VM *clients* (the browser console) are cross-platform.

---

## 📊 Performance Benchmarking

Moonshine is built for speed. Use the built-in shootout tool to compare Moonshine against legacy shells and modern runtimes.

```bash
# Run the internal benchmark
moonshine benchmark "echo hi" --runs 100

# Run the comparative shootout
moonshine benchmark shootout --runs 10
```

### Typical Results (Mean Latency)
| Runtime | Builtin (`echo`) | Math (`fib 20`) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Moonshine** | **0.3 ms** | **0.3 ms** |
| **zsh** | 24.0 ms | — |
| **Bun** | 29.0 ms | 38.0 ms |
| **Node** | 97.0 ms | 207.0 ms |

Facet operations stay on the shell side: fork/switch/close-active are O(1);
close-children is O(descendants); close-others is O(open facets) because it is a
pruning command.

---

## ⚙️ Configuration

Moonshine cascades configuration and environment variables from the root of the workspace down to the current directory.

- **`.moonshine.knot`**: Signed TOML/JSON defining aliases and permissions.
- **`.env`**: Standard environment variables.
- **`.env.knot`**: Signed, encrypted environment variables (Identity-locked).

### Example `.moonshine.knot`
```toml
[identity]
did = "did:key:z6MkhaX..."

[aliases]
ll = "ls -la"
build = "cargo build --release"
```

---

## 📁 Special Files & Environment

Moonshine reads several special files and environment variables. This section documents every one, matched to the source (`src/main.rs`, `src/agent_runtime.rs`, `src/executor.rs`).

### Config files: `.moonshine.knot` / `.moonshine.json` / `aeon.toml`

`Config::load` resolves the first existing file in this **search order**:

1. `~/.moonshine.knot`
2. `~/.moonshine.json`
3. `~/aeon.toml`
4. `./.moonshine.knot` (current directory)
5. `./.moonshine.json`
6. `./aeon.toml`

Home-directory files win over the current directory. The `-f`/`--config <FILE>` flag overrides the search entirely and loads exactly that path.

**Parsing rule:** a file is parsed as TOML when its extension is `.toml` **or** its name is `aeon.toml`; everything else (including the `.knot` files, despite the extension) is parsed as **JSON**. So `.moonshine.knot` and `.moonshine.json` must contain JSON; only `aeon.toml` is TOML. (Note: the README's Configuration example above and `moonshine init`'s output describe the schema, but `init` actually writes JSON into `.moonshine.knot`.)

**Schema:**

```jsonc
{
  "identity": { "did": "did:key:...", "secret": "..." },
  "aliases":  { "ll": "ls -F", "grep": "grep --color=auto" },
  "agent": {
    "llm_knot":             "~/.edgework/models/qwen2.5-0.5b-instruct.knot",
    "pneuma_acoustic_knot": "open-source/bitwise/datasets/pneuma_acoustic.knot",
    "pneuma_vocoder_knot":  "open-source/bitwise/datasets/pneuma_vocoder.knot"
  },
  "signed_env_only": false
}
```

- `identity.did` / `identity.secret` — DID and HMAC secret used to sign session UCANs and to verify `.env.knot` signatures.
- `aliases` — first-word command expansions.
- `agent` — knot paths for the agent runtime (see Knots below). **An absent `agent` block falls back to code defaults** (each field also defaults individually if omitted).
- `signed_env_only` — when `true`, unsigned `.env` files are skipped with a warning (only `.env.knot` is honored).

If no config file is found at all, Moonshine uses built-in defaults (`did:key:z6MkqMoonshineDefault`, a placeholder secret, default agent knots, no aliases).

### Environment files: `.env` and `.env.knot`

Environment is loaded **CSS-style cascading** from the filesystem root down to the current directory: directories are visited root-first so that **deeper directories override shallower ones** (cwd wins). In each directory:

- `.env.knot` is checked first. It is a **signed, identity-locked** env file. Its signature is verified (HMAC-SHA256 over `kind` + payload, signer must equal the config DID); if valid, its payload keys are loaded and the plain `.env` in that directory is skipped.
- `.env` (plain `KEY=value` lines, `#` comments allowed) is loaded only if there is no valid `.env.knot` — and only when `signed_env_only` is `false`. With `signed_env_only = true`, an unsigned `.env` is skipped with a warning.

`.env.knot` shape (the payload is a flat string map; each payload key becomes an env var, e.g. `Cascading=Signed`):

```json
{
  "kind": "environment",
  "payload": { "Cascading": "Signed" },
  "signature": "o2f_AYAjMrQYOJsB0QzXp9IvMW8yb24nUB_RetwyGis",
  "signer": "did:key:z6MkqMoonshineKnot"
}
```

`moonshine doctor` reports whether `.env` and `.env.knot` exist in the current directory.

### Knots (model weights)

- **`llm_knot`** drives the agent and one-off runtimes. The default (`default_llm_knot`) resolves in this precedence:
  1. `MOONSHINE_LLM_KNOT` env var, if set.
  2. `~/.edgework/models/qwen2.5-0.5b-instruct.knot` if it exists (lightweight 0.5B, ~2s forward).
  3. `~/.edgework/models/qwen-coder-7b.knot` if it exists.
  4. Fallback: `open-source/bitwise/datasets/qwen2.5-0.5b-instruct-q4_k_m.knot`.

  When warming a fat-station, Moonshine reads the **transformer layer count from the knot's `KNOT` header** (`config.num_layers`) and passes `--layers 0..N` (0.5B = 24, coder-7B = 28; defaults to 28 if the header can't be read).
- **`pneuma_acoustic_knot`** — default `open-source/bitwise/datasets/pneuma_acoustic.knot`.
- **`pneuma_vocoder_knot`** — default `open-source/bitwise/datasets/pneuma_vocoder.knot`.

### Environment variables

| Variable | Purpose |
| :--- | :--- |
| `MOONSHINE_LLM_KNOT` | Overrides the default LLM knot path (highest precedence). |
| `MOONSHINE_TOKENIZER_GGUF` | Explicit GGUF whose Qwen-family vocab backs the fat-station's `POST /detokenize` (honored verbatim, no existence check). Falls back to `~/.edgework/models/Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf`, then a deepseek-r1-qwen3-8b GGUF, if present. |
| `TOKENIZER_GGUF_PATH` | The env var Moonshine **sets on the fat-station process** to the resolved GGUF, so `/detokenize` returns real text (e.g. `Paris`) instead of token ids. If nothing resolves, it is left unset and the station uses its tiny smoke table. |
| `FAT_STATION_PORT` | Port for the fat-station inference server. Default `8000`. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_GEN_TOKENS` | Max generated tokens for shell fallback and `agent chat` turns handed to the local fat-client bridge. Default `256`; lower it for quick smoke runs or raise it for longer coding answers. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_TOOL_LOOP` | Kill-switch for the model-driven tool loop in the agent REPL and `agentic exec --provider sovereign`. Default on; set `0` to restore the legacy single-turn chat + human `/tool` dispatch exactly. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_BRAIN` | Brain for agent-loop decision turns: `auto` (default: skymesh first, fat-station fallback when `:8000` is warm), `skymesh`, `fat-station`, or `ssm`. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_CHAT_URL` / `_CHAT_MODEL` / `_CHAT_TOKENS` / `_CHAT_RETRIES` | Skymesh endpoint config for the agent-loop brain. Defaults mirror browser-step: `https://skymesh.forkjoin.ai/v1/chat/completions`, model `15b`, 256 tokens, 3 retries. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS` / `MOONSHINE_AGENT_MAX_TOOL_CALLS` | Loop budgets per user turn. Defaults `6` / `4`. `--max-turns` on `agentic exec` overrides the former. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_TOOL_CANDIDATES` | How many MCP tools are offered to the model per turn (registry search first, lexical fallback; `network/search` always included). Default `6`; capped at 3 on the fat-station brain. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_TOOL_TIMEOUT_SECS` | `tools/call` timeout against the MCP superserver (federated backends fan out over the network). Default `45`. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_SECS` | `initialize`/`tools/list` timeout against the MCP superserver. A cold superserver enumerates every federated backend on first contact and can take 10-15s; a warm one answers in milliseconds. Default `20`. |
| `MOONSHINE_MCP_URL` | Full MCP endpoint URL (including path). Default `http://127.0.0.1:8800/mcp`. |
| `MOONSHINE_AGENT_TOOL_RESULT_CHARS` | Truncation for tool results fed back to the model. Default `2000` (`600` on the fat-station brain — its per-token prompt feed makes every character wall-clock). |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_RETRY` | `browser-step` retries once with the validation reason appended when the model emits an invalid action (skymesh/SSM brains only). Default on; set `0` to disable. |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_STRICT_ERRORS` | Set `1` to make `browser-step` failures emit `{"action":"error","reason":…}` instead of the Kenoma-compatible `done` fallback. Default off — Kenoma's actor drops unknown actions. |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_CHAT_URL` | `browser-step` decision endpoint. Default `https://skymesh.forkjoin.ai/v1/chat/completions` (sovereign mesh zoo, OpenAI-compatible). |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_CHAT_MODEL` | Mesh zoo model id for `browser-step`. Default `15b` (DeepSeek-R1-1.5B — the measured speed/quality middle ground, ~4.8s/forward + coherent; a reasoner, so its `<think>` block is stripped before parsing). Alternatives: `q3` (Qwen2.5-3B, slower but no thinking), `coder`, `qwen3`. |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_CHAT_TOKENS` | Max completion tokens for a `browser-step` action. Default `256` (room for the reasoner's think+answer; models stop at EOS so this does not slow non-reasoners). |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_CHAT_RETRIES` | Retries on a flaky mesh station (transient `unreachable`/5xx). Default `3`. |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_LOCAL` | Set to `1` to make `browser-step` use the local fat-station per-token path instead of the skymesh mesh (offline/debug). The mesh is the default; the agent no longer needs a warm `:8000`. |
| `RUN_CLOUD_RUN_TESTS` | Must be exactly `true` before `browser-step` will use a Cloud Run SSM endpoint. |
| `MOONSHINE_BROWSER_SSM_URL` | Diagnostic RWKV-7 SSM endpoint. Ignored unless `RUN_CLOUD_RUN_TESTS=true`, so normal browser-step runs do not create Cloud Run traffic. |
| `MOONSHINE_UCAN` | Fallback UCAN token used in the interactive REPL when `--ucan` is not passed. |
| `MOONSHINE_VERBOSE` | Set to `1` automatically when `--verbose` is passed; enables verbose logging. |

(The agent runtime also reads `GNODE_JS_PATH` to locate `gnode.js` when warming the MCP superserver on port 8800.)

### Scripts Moonshine executes

Passed as the positional argument or recognized inline:

- **`.gnot`** — Graph-Oriented Notation flow files, compiled to a CFG and executed.
- **`.gnarly`** — advanced GG-family multilingual topologies (see the orchestration section below).

### CLI flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| :--- | :--- |
| `-c, --command <CMD>` | Execute a single command string (or the prompt for `--oneoff`). |
| `-f, --config <FILE>` | Load this config file, bypassing the search order. |
| `-m, --mock` | Mock mode — no side effects. |
| `-v, --verbose` | Verbose logging (sets `MOONSHINE_VERBOSE=1`). |
| `-u, --ucan <TOKEN>` | UCAN token for authorization. |
| `--oneoff` | One-shot real mesh generation (see below); requires `-c <prompt>`. |
| `--oneoff-tokens <N>` | Number of tokens to generate in `--oneoff` mode (default `8`). |

### `--oneoff`: one-shot mesh generation

`--oneoff` warms (or reuses) a fat-station with the configured `llm_knot`, runs **one real mesh generation** (`POST /tokenize` -> repeated `POST /decode-next` to prefill the prompt and autoregress), detokenizes the result via `POST /detokenize`, prints it, and exits. It skips the MCP superserver and the interactive REPL.

```bash
moonshine --oneoff -c "The capital of France is"
```

Sample output:

```text
--- Moonshine One-Off Generation ---
[oneoff] fat-station port: 8000
[oneoff] llm_knot: ~/.edgework/models/qwen2.5-0.5b-instruct.knot
[oneoff] prompt: "The capital of France is"
[oneoff] generating 8 token(s)
...
------------------------------------
The capital of France is -> Paris
```

If no tokenizer GGUF resolves, the station has no real vocab and the run prints raw token ids instead of text.

---

## `agent spawn`: user-spawnable agents

Moonshine makes the **human a first-class agent spawner** — a peer to Thoth.
Thoth grows its swarm autonomically over MCP; the user spawns agents directly
from the shell, under their own UCAN, through the same guarded path.

```bash
moonshine agent spawn --node <bin> [--oath <file>] [--qspec <file>] [--caps ...] [-- <args>]
```

`moonshine agent spawn` **shells to `monster-swarm spawn`** under the user's own
capability, so the spawn is double-gated (UCAN + FRF budget), optionally
**quality-gated** (`--qspec`, *works*), and optionally **oath-bound** (`--oath`,
*conduct* — bound into the grant as the UCAN `fct`). An agent is one definition
regardless of who minted it: a leased process + its own DID + a passed qspec + a
sworn oath.

### UCAN resolution

The user's spawn authority is resolved, in order:

1. `MOONSHINE_AGENT_UCAN` env var, if set.
2. `~/.edgework/agent.ucan`.

### Bootstrap

Mint the user's spawn capability once, using monster-swarm's helper:

```bash
# keygen + monster-resident grant --with agent:fleet --can agent/spawn
# -> writes ~/.edgework/agent.ucan
open-source/gnosis/monster-swarm/scripts/grant-user.sh
```

After bootstrap, every `moonshine agent spawn` runs under that `agent/spawn`
capability on `agent:fleet`. Reference oaths live at
`open-source/gnosis/monster-swarm/oaths/*.oath.json`. Full operator detail —
the oathspec format, the `delegate --oath` caps/ttl bound + fct binding, and the
fleet-frame surfacing — is in the
[`MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK`](../MONSTER_AGENTIC_RUNBOOK.md#oaths-and-user-spawnable-agents)
([monster-swarm](../monster-swarm/README.md#user-spawnable),
[monster-guard](../monster-guard/README.md#oath-bound-admission)).

---

## 🧪 Advanced: .gnot and .gnarly Orchestration

Moonshine can execute `.gnot` files and `.gnarly` GG-family multilingual
topologies. `.gnarly` files use the shared Gnot parser for metadata, embedded
implementation blocks, and optional `sources = {context, mount, include,
exclude}` import packaging.

```bash
moonshine deploy_cluster.gnot
moonshine image_pipeline.gnarly
```

---

## 📜 Commands Reference

- `moonshine -c "<cmd>"`: Execute a command string.
- `moonshine init`: Initialize identity and defaults.
- `moonshine doctor`: Run environment diagnostics.
- `moonshine benchmark shootout`: Run the performance shootout.
- `moonshine shadow "<cmd>"`: Execute in shadowed/encrypted mode.
- `moonshine agent spawn --node <bin> [--oath <f>] [--qspec <f>] [--caps ...] [-- <args>]`: Spawn an agent under your own UCAN (see [`agent spawn`](#agent-spawn-user-spawnable-agents)).

---

## 🛡 Security & Authorization

Moonshine is **UCAN-native**.
Use the `--ucan` flag to pass a capability token. The engine verifies the signature and the specific resource permissions before spawning any process.

```bash
moonshine -c "rm -rf /" --ucan "eyJhbG..." # Will fail without explicit permission
```

---

## 🤝 Contributing

Run the DX acceptance suite before submitting changes:
```bash
cargo test
cargo clippy
```

---

"Moonshine is the ghost in the shell, if the shell was a prism."
